title: Bibimbap

overview.description: A colourful Korean rice bowl topped with seasoned vegetables, marinated beef, a fried egg and a spicy gochujang sauce. Mix everything together before eating for a beautifully balanced meal.

ingredients: 500g beef steak (sirloin or rump), thinly sliced
method: Cook the rice according to the packet instructions and keep warm.
Mix the beef with the soy sauce, sesame oil, garlic and sugar. Leave to marinate for about 20 minutes.
Lightly cook each vegetable separately using a little vegetable oil so each keeps its own colour and texture.
Cook the marinated beef in a very hot frying pan or wok until just cooked through.
Fry the eggs until the whites are set but the yolks remain runny.
Divide the rice between four bowls.
Arrange the vegetables and beef in neat sections over the rice.
Top each bowl with a fried egg.
Serve sprinkled with spring onions, sesame seeds and the gochujang sauce.

learning.did-you-know: Bibimbap literally means 'mixed rice'. Although it's beautifully arranged when served, it's traditionally stirred together before eating.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to prepare several simple components, cook vegetables individually to preserve their flavour, and balance textures in one bowl.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A fantastic way to introduce Korean cooking while encouraging plenty of vegetables. It also teaches preparation and organisation rather than difficult cooking techniques.
chapman-tips: Cook each vegetable separately so every ingredient keeps its own flavour.
Runny egg yolks become part of the sauce when mixed through.
Don't skip the gochujang—it gives the dish its distinctive character.
notes.batch-cooking: Prepare the vegetables and beef ahead of time, then assemble fresh.
notes.leftovers: Excellent for lunch the following day, although the egg is best cooked fresh.
